question_text: By 2050, nearly how many people are projected to be living with some degree of hearing loss, per WHO?
At least how many people will require rehabilitation services for hearing loss by 2050?
How many people currently have moderate or higher levels of hearing loss requiring rehabilitation services, per WHO?
Do hearing aids cure hearing loss?
What do hearing technologies like hearing aids and cochlear implants require to ensure desired outcomes?
ground_truth_answer: Appropriate support services and rehabilitative therapy

question_text: Can noise exposure cause hearing loss in addition to age-related causes?
Name two causative factors of hearing loss besides aging and noise exposure.
ground_truth_answer: Otitis media (ear infections) and ototoxic medicines

question_text: What percentage of hearing loss in children is due to causes that can be prevented?
How many young people worldwide are at risk of avoidable hearing loss, per WHO?
Approximately how many people suffer from preventable or treatable chronic ear infections, per WHO?
What is the estimated annual global cost of unaddressed hearing loss?
For every 1 dollar invested in ear and hearing care, what return can governments expect?
What percentage of people who could benefit from a hearing aid actually use one?
What hearing threshold, in decibels, is used to define clinical hearing loss?
In low-income countries, what percentage have fewer than one ear, nose, and throat specialist per million population?
What does the acronym H.E.A.R.I.N.G. stand for in WHO's framework for ear and hearing care?
ground_truth_answer: Hearing screening and intervention; Ear disease prevention and management; Access to technologies; Rehabilitation services; Improved communication; Noise reduction; Greater community engagement

question_text: By how much might the number of people with hearing loss increase over the next three decades from 2019 levels?
What is the projected number of people with disabling hearing loss by 2050, according to WHO's chart?
Can hearing loss negatively affect a child's development of language and speech if unaddressed?
What additional annual per-capita investment is needed to scale up ear and hearing care globally by 2030?
What is the title of this NIOSH Safety and Health Information Bulletin?
ground_truth_answer: Preventing Hearing Loss Caused by Chemical (Ototoxicity) and Noise Exposure

question_text: What are ototoxicants?
ground_truth_answer: Chemicals, such as certain pesticides, solvents, and pharmaceuticals, that can cause hearing loss or balance problems regardless of noise exposure

question_text: Can combined exposure to noise and ototoxic chemicals cause greater hearing loss than either exposure alone?
What are the three categories used to classify ototoxic chemicals based on which part of the ear they damage?
Name two classes of substances that can be ototoxic.
At what noise action level does OSHA's occupational noise exposure standard require audiometric testing?
Can hearing loss occur from ototoxic chemical exposure even when noise levels are below OSHA's Permissible Exposure Limit?
Name two industries identified as more likely to have ototoxicant exposure.
What is the first recommended step in preventing exposure to ototoxicants in the workplace?
ground_truth_answer: Reviewing Safety Data Sheets (SDS) to identify whether ototoxicants are present

question_text: Exposure to ototoxic chemicals can cause difficulty with which four types of auditory processing?
ground_truth_answer: Compressed loudness, frequency resolution, temporal resolution, and spatial resolution

question_text: Is noise-induced hearing loss from damaged sensory hair cells always permanent?
ground_truth_answer: No, it can be either temporary or permanent, depending on the level of noise, dose of chemical, and duration of exposure

question_text: What are the three benchmarks in CDC's EHDI 1-3-6 plan?
ground_truth_answer: Screening before 1 month of age, diagnostic evaluation before 3 months of age, and early intervention enrollment before 6 months of age

question_text: By what age should all infants be screened for hearing loss?
If an infant does not pass the initial hearing screening, by when should they receive a full diagnostic evaluation?
By what age should infants with confirmed hearing loss be enrolled in early intervention services?
What government center is the source of the EHDI 1-3-6 benchmarks?
ground_truth_answer: National Center on Birth Defects and Developmental Disabilities (NCBDDD)

question_text: Providing recommended services to infants with hearing loss before 6 months helps them develop what skills?
ground_truth_answer: Communication and language skills that will last a lifetime

question_text: What percentage of the U.S. adult population has experienced tinnitus lasting at least five minutes in the past year?
Approximately how many Americans have experienced tinnitus, per NIDCD?
How many drugs are known to potentially cause tinnitus as a side effect?
What is pulsatile tinnitus?
ground_truth_answer: A rare type of tinnitus that sounds like a rhythmic pulsing in the ear, usually in time with the heartbeat, often caused by blood flow problems in the head or neck

question_text: Name two health conditions, besides noise-induced hearing loss, that can cause tinnitus.
Is tinnitus one of the most common service-related disabilities among veterans returning from Iraq and Afghanistan?
In what year did NIDCD sponsor a workshop bringing together tinnitus researchers to discuss potential cures?
What does acoustic neural stimulation use to help treat tinnitus?
ground_truth_answer: A palm-sized device and headphones that deliver a broadband acoustic signal embedded in music

question_text: Can antidepressants or antianxiety drugs be part of tinnitus treatment?
ground_truth_answer: Yes, to improve mood and help with sleep

question_text: Do hearing aids cure tinnitus, or do they help manage it?
ground_truth_answer: They help manage it by improving hearing, which can make tinnitus less noticeable, but they do not cure it

question_text: Is tinnitus generally considered a disease or a symptom?
ground_truth_answer: A symptom, not a disease itself

question_text: Can tinnitus be permanently cured with current treatments?
What are two examples of sound-masking devices used to manage tinnitus?
ground_truth_answer: Wearable sound generators and tabletop sound generators

question_text: Since tinnitus has no cure, what management approaches are used, and what do they aim to achieve?
ground_truth_answer: Hearing aids, counseling, sound generators, acoustic neural stimulation, cochlear implants, and medications; they aim to reduce the severity and impact of tinnitus rather than eliminate the sound
